senses.definition: to check a web page, text, etc. without  reading all the content

examples.x: He browsed online forums to read reviews and opinions about the latest video games.
He browsed the internet for hours, looking for information on his favorite topic.
She likes to browse online stores for hours, searching for the perfect gifts.
He browsed various news websites to stay updated on current events.
We browsed the web for restaurant reviews before deciding where to dine out.

senses.definition: to buy or sell an item that has been auctioned on eBay
(computing) to remove any information from the data sent or received over the internet so that it cannot be traced or identified
to store the address of a file, website, etc. for faster and easier access
to search the Internet for information about someone or something using the Google search engine

examples.x: Don't hesitate to google for solutions to common problems.
He googled the definition of a new word.
She googled the answer to her math question.
The team googled the history of the ancient civilization.
You can google travel tips for your upcoming trip.

senses.definition: to read the posts on a social media platform or discussion forum without actively participating
(computing) to move from one website to another or find one's way around on a website

examples.x: Students were taught how to navigate the educational platform to access course materials.
During the online workshop, participants were guided on how to navigate the webinar platform.
Users can navigate the social media platform to connect with friends, share updates, and explore trending topics.
The job seeker navigated the career portal, searching for relevant job postings.

senses.definition: to make something more useful or modern by adding the most recent information to it, improving its faults, or making new features available for it

examples.x: She decided to update her resume with her most recent job experience.
They needed to update the kitchen appliances to match the modern decor.
The website undergoes regular maintenance to update its content and improve user experience.
I updated my calendar with the latest meeting times and events.
The article was updated to include new research findings.

senses.definition: to send an electronic file such as a document, image, etc. from one digital device to another one, often by using the Internet

examples.x: After editing the photo, she will upload it to her online portfolio.
Please upload your assignment to the online platform before the deadline.
The students were asked to upload their presentations to the class website.
The website allows users to upload their own videos and share them with others.
They will upload the recording of the webinar for those who missed it.

senses.definition: to add data to a computer from the Internet or another computer

examples.x: She downloaded the app to keep track of her daily activities.
She's excited to download the new game on her computer.
The app allows users to download videos for offline viewing.
The website allows users to download free music.
You can download the document by clicking the link.

senses.definition: to search the internet for one's own name or a personal website
to enter a username and password in order to gain access to a website, computer, etc.

examples.x: After you sign in, you'll have access to exclusive content.
He was unable to sign in because he forgot his password.
Please sign in with your username and password to access the website.
The website asks users to sign in for personalized recommendations.
We encourage all participants to sign in upon arrival.

senses.definition: to exit a computer account such as email, Instagram, etc. in a way that you have to re-enter your username and password in order to use it again

examples.x: Don't forget to sign out when you're done using the computer.
He accidentally left the account signed in and had to sign out remotely.
The security policy requires employees to sign out before leaving the office.

senses.definition: to start using a computer system, online account, or application by doing particular actions

examples.x: You need to log in to your bank account to transfer money.
The company's policy mandates that employees log in before using the network.
I need to log in to my work computer to access my files.
The student logged in to the online learning platform to take their test.
The employee was unable to log in because he forgot his password.

senses.definition: to stop a connection to an online account or computer system by doing specific actions

examples.x: I need to log off my work computer before leaving for the day.
I always log off my social media accounts before closing my browser.
The individual logged off their personal computer to secure their privacy.

senses.definition: used on the internet to acknowledge someone's role in providing information or giving an idea
to share content online and make it available to others

examples.x: After editing the video, she was ready to publish it on her YouTube channel.
They publish new episodes of their podcast every Friday for subscribers.
The writer chose to publish the e-book on multiple platforms to increase its visibility.
After hours of work, he was eager to publish his project online for feedback.
He publishes his photography portfolio online to showcase his work to potential clients.

senses.definition: to explore content or information on the internet or in other media without a specific goal

examples.x: After work, she likes to relax by surfing her favorite social media sites.
During the project, students were encouraged to surf online databases to gather information.
In the mall, we decided to surf different stores, looking for the perfect gift.
Unable to decide on a book to read, he spent the afternoon surfing his bookshelf.
Instead of watching a specific show, I prefer to surf through TV channels and see what's on.

senses.definition: (computing) to update a display, internet page, etc. and make the most recent information appear

examples.x: You need to refresh the browser to view the updated content.
After the new post appeared, she refreshed her feed to see the latest updates.
He refreshed the page, and the new comments showed up instantly.
The website didn’t load correctly, so I had to refresh it several times.
Press F5 to refresh the page and view the updated results.

senses.definition: to regularly write in a blog or add content to it

examples.x: She blogs about her daily life and travels.
They blog together about their culinary experiments.
She blogs every week to keep her readers updated.

senses.definition: to write and share a real time commentary of an event online in a blog as it is happening
to play audio or video material from the Internet without needing to download the whole file on one's device

examples.x: They decided to stream the latest episode of their favorite TV show instead of waiting for it to download.
He prefers to stream music online rather than purchase physical CDs.
The platform allows users to stream movies in high definition directly to their devices.
She set up her computer to stream live sports events from around the world.
He streams video games on Twitch for his followers.
